contractSurface.securityClassification: tenant-scoped CMS authoring, approval, scheduling, public publishing, and analytics controls
coreGuarantees: Content publishing keeps tenant ownership checks before publication.
Optimistic concurrency protects draft-to-published state transitions.
Approval stages remain sequential and role-gated.
Scheduled publishing uses a per-content idempotency lock.
Schedule cancellation records audit evidence before downstream events.
Certified author PII fields remain excluded from analytics metadata.
Tenant context resolution remains automatic through TenantContextResolver.

packageSigningConstraint.reason: FLOW-22 can publish public tenant content and route role-gated editorial decisions, so Tier 3 requires package-signing first pass.

machineLockedRules: content tenant must match request tenant before publication
audit record is stored before ContentPublished is emitted
approval stage must be a recognized stage
approval audit is stored before ContentApproved or ContentRejected is emitted
publishAt must be a future timestamp
schedule lock key is content and timestamp specific
duplicate lock returns the existing scheduled result
schedule audit is stored before ContentScheduled is emitted
metric type must be one of VIEW, ENGAGEMENT, or SHARE
authorEmail, authorIpAddress, and editorNotes are excluded from analytics metadata
analytics record is stored before AnalyticsRecorded is emitted

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-22 CMS operating values. These values may vary by tenant without changing content ownership checks, published-version immutability, OCC, sequential approvals, schedule idempotency, audit-before-event ordering, append-only analytics, certified PII exclusions, or tenant isolation.

keys.semantic: Maximum UI-side retry guidance for a draft publish conflict.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune author workflow messaging while the OCC conflict rule remains fixed.

keys.semantic: Default audience mode suggested when a new CMS item is prepared for publishing.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune editorial defaults while runtime access enforcement remains role checked.

keys.semantic: Named approval stages expected in the tenant editorial workflow.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may add or keep compliance review stages while sequential approval remains fixed.

keys.semantic: Target editorial review time used in tenant dashboards and alerts.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune review expectations without bypassing approval gates.

keys.semantic: Time-to-live for a scheduled publish lock.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune scheduling windows while exactly-once schedule locks remain fixed.

keys.semantic: Timezone interpretation policy for editorial schedule displays.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may choose display policy while stored publish timestamps stay ISO based.

keys.semantic: Retention window for tenant CMS analytics records.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune reporting history while append-only storage and PII exclusions remain fixed.

keys.semantic: Default reporting granularity for content engagement metrics.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune reporting views while tenant scoped analytics remain fixed.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to editorial defaults, approval SLA posture, schedule-window policy, analytics retention, and customer-facing module naming.

edgeCases.name: Public help center publishing
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A defaults CMS items to public help-center content with shorter publish-conflict retry guidance.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T633 still checks tenant ownership, OCC, and published-version immutability.

edgeCases.name: Compliance review path
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A keeps legal review and uses a shorter editorial SLA for operational content.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T634 still validates known stages and writes audit records before events.

edgeCases.name: Tenant-local schedule policy
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A keeps tenant-local schedule displays and extends the schedule lock window for campaign pages.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T635 still requires future timestamps and keeps idempotency locks.

edgeCases.name: Lean content analytics
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A shortens retention and keeps weekly rollups for help-center analytics.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T636 still excludes certified PII fields and writes append-only records.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T633 through T636 remain fixed.
Factory IDs F1577 through F1583 remain fixed.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
Tenant ownership check remains mandatory before publishing.
OCC remains mandatory for publish state transitions.
Approval stages remain recognized and sequential.
Schedule locks remain content and timestamp specific.
Schedule timestamps must be future timestamps.
Audit records remain stored before downstream events.
authorEmail, authorIpAddress, and editorNotes remain excluded from analytics metadata.
Tenant context resolution remains automatic through TenantContextResolver.

tenantAHelpCenterPublishing.description: Tenant A tunes CMS Publishing for public help-center content and faster editorial operations.
tenantAHelpCenterPublishing.impact: Tenant A gets help-center publishing defaults, faster review expectations, longer campaign scheduling locks, and leaner analytics retention while ownership checks, immutable publish state, OCC, audit ordering, append-only analytics, PII exclusions, and tenant isolation stay unchanged.
tenantAHelpCenterPublishing.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-22 configuration values or display/module metadata.
All four services remain MicroserviceBase subclasses and use TenantContextResolver for tenant scope.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
